text:
🚨 BREAKING: Bosch Research just published a paper that explains why most production AI systems are flying blind.

It's called "Full Traceability and Provenance for Knowledge Graphs."
The core finding: systems that can't trace what changed, when, and why cannot learn from https://t.co/pJofc5wDs3

Truly blown away by a new AI image model launching this week ✨

Finally, you can generate photos that actually look like you! 

It's so much better than everything I've tried - from LoRAs to NB Pro.

Onboarding some early testers. DM or comment if you want access 👀 https://t.co/xAUXJjtvVS

78K tradespeople short

More charts: https://t.co/Bf9GsbzeVf https://t.co/0sJhBTAYs6

12% fewer 20-somethings in the workforce since 1976

More charts: https://t.co/Bf9GsbzeVf https://t.co/1wJ3e6vtGY

Guess the AI video model 
while my mind is blown 🤯 https://t.co/zw1O5203X1

The idea that technology, like AI, deskills us is not a surprise. I learned cursive at school, my father learned how to use a slide rule. Neither skill is widely mourned.

What is important is whether we will make deliberate choices about what skills to keep & which they will be.
(And whether we will have the discipline to actually keep doing the grinding work needed to build expertise.)

I hate the AI comments every post of mine attracts.

Worse than the crypto swarms of 2020 or the weird viral marketing bots or even the stupid “first” memes of early commentary. 

At least then it was us against them, now everyone is pretending to be a human & wasting your time.

RT @godofprompt: 🚨 BREAKING: University of Tartu just quantified exactly how badly the AI industry is measuring model uncertainty.

I will start getting back to everyone as soon as it's back up! Excited by all the interest.
